name: rtrec

metadata.name: rtrec
metadata.summary: An realtime recommendation system supporting online updates

documentation:
# rtrec
An realtime recommendation system supporting online updates

modules.name: rtrec.models
documentation: from rtrec._lowlevel import SlimMSE as Fast_SLIM_MSE

methods.name: rtrec.models.slim.SLIM.bulk_fit
documentation:
Fit the recommender model on the given interaction matrix.
\:param interaction_matrix: Sparse interaction matrix
\:param parallel: Whether to run the fitting process in parallel. Defaults to False
\:param progress_bar: Whether to display a progress bar

methods.name: rtrec.models.slim.SLIM._recommend
documentation:
Recommend top-K items for a given user.
\:param user_id: User index
\:param candidate_item_ids: List of candidate item indices
\:param top_k: Number of top items to recommend
\:param filter_interacted: Whether to filter out items the user has already interacted with
\:return: List of top-K item indices recommended for the user

methods.name: rtrec.models.slim.SLIM._similar_items
documentation:
Find similar items for a list of query items.
\:param query_item_ids: List of query item indices
\:param top_k: Number of top similar items to return for each query item
\:param filter_query_items: Whether to filter out items in the query_items list
\:return: List of top-K similar items for each query item

classes.name: rtrec.models.internal.lightfm_wrapper.LightFMWrapper

methods.name: rtrec.models.internal.lightfm_wrapper.LightFMWrapper.fit_partial
documentation:
Fit the model on a new batch of interactions, user and item features.
This method resizes the model to accomodate new users, items, and features.

Parameters
----------
interactions : coo_matrix
    The user-item interactions matrix of shape [n_users, n_items].
user_features : csr_matrix, optional
    The user features matrix of shape [n_users, n_user_features].
item_features : csr_matrix, optional
    The item features matrix of shape [n_items, n_item_features].
sample_weight : coo_matrix, optional
    The sample weights matrix of shape [n_users, n_items].
epochs : int, optional
    The number of epochs to run.
num_threads : int, optional
    The number of threads to use.
verbose : bool, optional
    Whether to print progress information.

methods.name: rtrec.models.internal.lightfm_wrapper.LightFMWrapper._resize
documentation: Resizes the model to accommodate new users/items/features

A LightFM that resizes the model to accomodate new users, items, and features.

See https://github.com/lyst/lightfm/issues/347#issuecomment-707829342

methods.name: rtrec.models.internal.implicit_fm.ImplicitFactorizationMachines.__init__
documentation:
Factorization Machines for implicit feedback.

Args:
    num_factors (int): Number of latent factors.
    learning_rate (float): Learning rate for updates.
    reg (float): Regularization parameter.
    loss (str): Loss function to use ('bpr' or 'warp').
    random_state (Optional[int]): Random seed for reproducibility.
    epsilon (float): Smoothing term for AdaGrad.

methods.name: rtrec.models.internal.implicit_fm.ImplicitFactorizationMachines.fit
documentation:
Fits the model to the provided interaction data.

Args:
    interactions (csr_matrix): User-item interaction matrix (implicit feedback).
    epochs (int): Number of training epochs.

methods.name: rtrec.models.internal.implicit_fm.ImplicitFactorizationMachines.partial_fit
documentation:
Incrementally fits the model to new interaction data, considering new users and items.

Args:
    interactions (csr_matrix): User-item interaction matrix (implicit feedback).
    users (np.ndarray): Array of user indices to update.
    items (np.ndarray): Array of item indices to update.

methods.name: rtrec.models.internal.implicit_fm.ImplicitFactorizationMachines._update_warp
documentation: Performs an update using the WARP (Weighted Approximate-Rank Pairwise) loss with AdaGrad.

methods.name: rtrec.models.internal.implicit_fm.ImplicitFactorizationMachines._warp_update
documentation: Performs a WARP update with AdaGrad.

methods.name: rtrec.models.internal.implicit_fm.ImplicitFactorizationMachines.predict
documentation:
Predicts the scores for a user and a set of items.

Args:
    user (int): User index.
    items (np.ndarray): Array of item indices.

Returns:
    np.ndarray: Predicted scores for the items.

methods.name: rtrec.models.internal.implicit_fm.ImplicitFactorizationMachines.predict_all
documentation:
Predicts the scores for all items for a given user.

Args:
    user (int): User index.

Returns:
    np.ndarray: Predicted scores for all items.

methods.name: rtrec.models.internal.implicit_fm.ImplicitFactorizationMachines.recommend
documentation:
Recommend top-K items for a given user.

Args:
    user_id (int): ID of the user (row index in interactions).
    interactions (csr_matrix): User-item interaction matrix.
    top_k (int): Number of recommendations to return.
    exclude_seen (bool): Whether to exclude items the user has already interacted with.

Returns:
    List of recommended item indices.

classes.name: rtrec.models.internal.slim_elastic.CSRMatrixWrapper

methods.name: rtrec.models.internal.slim_elastic.CSRMatrixWrapper.get_col
documentation:
Return the j-th column of the matrix.

Parameters
----------
j : int
    The column index.

Returns
-------
scipy.sparse.csc_matrix
    The j-th column of the matrix.

methods.name: rtrec.models.internal.slim_elastic.CSRMatrixWrapper.set_col
documentation:
Set the j-th column of the matrix to the given values.

Parameters
----------
j : int
    The column index.
values : ArrayLike
    The new values for the column.
CSRMatrixWrapper is a wrapper class for a CSR matrix that provides efficient access to columns.

methods.name: rtrec.models.internal.slim_elastic.CSCMatrixWrapper.get_col
documentation:
Return the j-th column of the matrix.

Parameters
----------
j : int
    The column index.

Returns
-------
scipy.sparse.spmatrix
    The j-th column of the matrix.

methods.name: rtrec.models.internal.slim_elastic.CSCMatrixWrapper.set_col
documentation:
Set the j-th column of the matrix to the given values.

Parameters
----------
j : int
    The column index.
values : ArrayLike
    The new values for the column.

classes.name: rtrec.models.internal.slim_elastic.SLIMElastic

methods.name: rtrec.models.internal.slim_elastic.SLIMElastic.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the SLIMElastic model.

Args:
    alpha (float): Regularization strength.
    l1_ratio (float): The ratio between L1 and L2 regularization.
    positive_only (bool): Whether to enforce positive coefficients.

methods.name: rtrec.models.internal.slim_elastic.SLIMElastic.fit
documentation:
Fit the SLIMElastic model to the interaction matrix.

Args:
    interaction_matrix (csc_matrix | csr_matrix): User-item interaction matrix (sparse).
    parallel (bool): Whether to use parallel processing for fitting.
    progress_bar (bool): Whether to show a progress bar during training.

methods.name: rtrec.models.internal.slim_elastic.SLIMElastic.fit_in_parallel
documentation:
Fit the SLIM ElasticNet model in parallel.

Args:
    interaction_matrix (sp.csc_matrix): Sparse interaction matrix.
    item_ids (ndarray): List of item indices to fit. If None, fit all items.
    progress_bar (bool): Whether to display a progress bar.
    chunk_size (int): Number of items per chunk for parallel processing.
    num_workers (int): Number of worker processes to use. Defaults to 70% of available CPU cores.

Returns:
    Self: The fitted SLIM ElasticNet model.

methods.name: rtrec.models.internal.slim_elastic.SLIMElastic._fit_items
documentation:
Worker function for fitting multiple items using shared memory.

Args:
    shared_data_name (str): Name of shared memory for `data`.
    shared_indices_name (str): Name of shared memory for `indices`.
    shared_indptr_name (str): Name of shared memory for `indptr`.
    shm_shapes (Tuple[Tuple[int, int], Tuple[int], Tuple[int]]): Shapes of the shared arrays (`data`, `indices`, `indptr`).
    matrix_shape (Tuple[int, int]): Shape of the full interaction matrix.
    items (List[int]): List of target column indices.
    alpha (float): Regularization strength.
    l1_ratio (float): ElasticNet mixing parameter.

Returns:
    Dict[int, Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]]: Mapping of item index to (non-zero indices, coefficients).

methods.name: rtrec.models.internal.slim_elastic.SLIMElastic.partial_fit
documentation:
Incrementally fit the SLIMElastic model with new or updated users.

Args:
    interaction_matrix (csr_matrix): user-item interaction matrix (sparse).
    user_ids (list): List of user indices that were updated.
    parallel (bool): Whether to use parallel processing for fitting.
    progress_bar (bool): Whether to show a progress bar during training.

methods.name: rtrec.models.internal.slim_elastic.SLIMElastic.partial_fit_items
documentation:
Incrementally fit the SLIMElastic model with new or updated items.

Args:
    interaction_matrix (csc_matrix | csr_matrix): user-item interaction matrix (sparse).
    updated_items (list): List of item indices that were updated.
    parallel (bool): Whether to use parallel processing for fitting.
    progress_bar (bool): Whether to show a progress bar during training.

methods.name: rtrec.models.internal.slim_elastic.SLIMElastic.predict
documentation:
Compute the predicted scores for a specific user across all items.

Args:
    user_id (int): The user ID (row index in interaction_matrix).
    interaction_matrix (csr_matrix): User-item interaction matrix.
    dense_output (bool): Whether to return a dense output.

Returns:
    numpy.ndarray: Predicted scores for the user across all items of shape (n_items,)

methods.name: rtrec.models.internal.slim_elastic.SLIMElastic.predict_selected
documentation:
Compute the predicted scores for a specific user and a subset of items.

Args:
    user_id (int): The user ID (row index in interaction_matrix).
    item_ids (List[int]): List of item indices to compute the predicted scores for.
    interaction_matrix (csr_matrix): User-item interaction matrix.
    dense_output (bool): Whether to return a dense output.

Returns:
    numpy.ndarray: Predicted scores for the user and selected items of shape (len(item_ids),)

methods.name: rtrec.models.internal.slim_elastic.SLIMElastic.predict_all
documentation:
Compute the predicted scores for all users and items.

Args:
    interaction_matrix (csr_matrix): User-item interaction matrix.
    dense_output (bool): Whether to return a dense output.

Returns:
    numpy.ndarray | scipy.sparse.csr_matrix: Predicted scores for all users and items.

methods.name: rtrec.models.internal.slim_elastic.SLIMElastic.recommend
documentation:
Recommend top-K items for a given user.

Args:
    user_id (int): ID of the user (row index in interaction_matrix).
    interaction_matrix (csr_matrix): User-item interaction matrix (sparse).
    candidate_item_ids (List[int]): List of candidate item indices to recommend from. If None, recommend from all items.
    top_k (int): Number of recommendations to return.
    filter_interacted (bool): Whether to exclude items the user has already interacted with. Ignored if candidate_item_ids is provided.
    dense_output (bool): Whether to return dense item IDs at prediction time.

Returns:
    List[int]: List of top-K item indices recommended for the user.

methods.name: rtrec.models.internal.slim_elastic.SLIMElastic._dense_topk_indicies
documentation:
Get the top-K indices for a given dense matrix.

Args:
    scores (ndarray): Dense matrix of scores.
    top_k (int): Number of top indices to retrieve.
    user_id (int): User index.
    interaction_matrix (csr_matrix): User-item interaction matrix.
    filter_interacted (bool): Whether to filter out items the user has already interacted with.

Returns:
    List[int]: List of top-K indices.

methods.name: rtrec.models.internal.slim_elastic.SLIMElastic._sparse_topk_indicies
documentation:
Get the top-K indices for a given sparse matrix.

Args:
    scores (csr_matrix): Sparse matrix of scores.
    top_k (int): Number of top indices to retrieve.
    filter_interacted (bool): Whether to filter out items the user has already interacted with.

Returns:
    List[int]: List of top-K indices.

methods.name: rtrec.models.internal.slim_elastic.SLIMElastic.similar_items
documentation:
Get the top-K most similar items to a given item.

Args:
    item_id (int): The item ID (column index in the interaction matrix).
    top_k (int): Number of similar items to retrieve.

Returns:
    List[int]: List of top-K similar item indices

SLIMElastic is a sparse linear method for top-K recommendation, which learns
a sparse aggregation coefficient matrix by solving an L1-norm and L2-norm
regularized optimization problem.

functions.name: rtrec.experiments.utils.n_core_filter
documentation:
Filters the DataFrame to only include rows where any specified column's values
appear at least `min_count` times.

Args:
    df (pd.DataFrame): The input DataFrame.
    columns (List[str]): The list of columns to apply the n-core filter on.
    min_count (int): Minimum occurrences required for each value in any specified column 
                     to be retained. Defaults to 10.

Returns:
    pd.DataFrame: A filtered DataFrame containing only rows where values in any specified 
                  column appear at least `min_count` times.

documentation: Define input models for requests

functions.name: rtrec.recommender.generate_batches
documentation:
Converts a DataFrame to an iterable of mini-batches.

Parameters:
    df (pd.DataFrame): The DataFrame to convert to mini-batches.
    batch_size (int): The number of rows per mini-batch.
    as_generator (bool): Whether to return a generator or a list of mini-batches.

Returns:
    Iterator[Iterable[Tuple[int, int, int, float]]]: An iterator of mini-batches.

methods.name: rtrec.recommender.Recommender.partial_fit
documentation: Incrementally fit the recommender model on new interactions.

methods.name: rtrec.recommender.Recommender.fit
documentation:
Fit the recommender model on the given DataFrame of interactions.
:param train_data (pd.DataFrame): The DataFrame containing interactions with columns (user, item, tstamp, rating).
:param batch_size (int): The number of interactions per mini-batch. Defaults to 1000.
:param update_interaction (bool): Whether to update existing interactions. Defaults to False.
:param parallel (bool): Whether to run the fitting process in parallel. Defaults to False.

methods.name: rtrec.recommender.Recommender.bulk_fit
documentation:
Fit the recommender model on the given DataFrame of interactions in a single batch.
:param train_data (pd.DataFrame): The DataFrame containing interactions with columns (user, item, tstamp, rating).
:param batch_size (int): The number of interactions per mini-batch. Defaults to 1000.
:param update_interaction (bool): Whether to update existing interactions. Defaults to False.
:param parallel (bool): Whether to run the fitting process in parallel. Defaults to True.

methods.name: rtrec.recommender.Recommender.recommend
documentation:
Recommend top-K items for a given user.
\:param user: User index
\:param top_k: Number of top items to recommend
\:param filter_interacted: Whether to filter out items the user has already interacted with
\:return: List of top-K item indices recommended for the user

documentation:
Recommend top-K items for a list of users.
\:param users: List of user indices

Find similar items for a list of query items.
\:param query_items: List of query items

methods.name: rtrec.recommender.Recommender.evaluate
documentation:
Evaluates the model using batch evaluation metrics on the test data.

Parameters:
    test_data (pd.DataFrame): DataFrame with columns ['user', 'item'] containing ground truth interactions.
    recommend_size (int): Number of items to recommend per user for evaluation.
    batch_size (int): Number of users to evaluate in each batch.
    filter_interacted (bool): Whether to filter out items the user has already interacted with during evaluation.

Returns:
    Dict[str, float]: Dictionary with averaged evaluation metrics across all users.

functions.name: rtrec.experiments.split.leave_one_last_item
documentation:
Perform a leave-one-last-item split on the dataset.

Parameters:
    df (pd.DataFrame): A pandas DataFrame with columns ['user', 'item', 'tstamp', 'rating'].

Returns:
    Tuple[pd.DataFrame, pd.DataFrame]: A tuple containing the training and test DataFrames.
        - train_df: Training data containing all but the last interaction per user.
        - test_df: Test data containing the last interaction per user.

functions.name: rtrec.experiments.split.temporal_split
documentation:
Perform a temporal split on the dataset based on either a specified test fraction or timestamp.

Parameters:
    df (pd.DataFrame): A pandas DataFrame with columns ['user', 'item', 'tstamp', 'rating'].
    test_frac (float): The proportion of data to include in the test set, between 0 and 1. 
                       Used if timestamp is not provided. Default is 0.2.
    timestamp (Optional[float]): The POSIX timestamp threshold for the split. Interactions before this
                                 timestamp will be in the training set, and interactions on or after
                                 this timestamp will be in the test set.

Returns:
    Tuple[pd.DataFrame, pd.DataFrame]: A tuple containing the training and test DataFrames.
        - train_df: Training data with interactions before the calculated split point or timestamp.
        - test_df: Test data with interactions on or after the calculated split point or timestamp.

functions.name: rtrec.experiments.split.temporal_user_split
documentation:
Perform a temporal split on the dataset for each user individually based on a specified test fraction.

Parameters:
    df (pd.DataFrame): A pandas DataFrame with columns ['user', 'item', 'tstamp', 'rating'].
    test_frac (float): The proportion of each user's data to include in the test set, between 0 and 1.
                       Default is 0.2.
    sort_by_tstamp (bool): Whether to sort the resulting DataFrames by timestamp. Default is True.

Returns:
    Tuple[pd.DataFrame, pd.DataFrame]: A tuple containing the training and test DataFrames.
        - train_df: Training data with interactions before the calculated split point for each user.
        - test_df: Test data with interactions on or after the calculated split point for each user.

functions.name: rtrec.experiments.split.random_split
documentation:
Perform a random split on the dataset based on a specified test fraction.

Parameters:
    df (pd.DataFrame): A pandas DataFrame with columns ['user', 'item', 'tstamp', 'rating'].
    test_frac (float): The proportion of data to include in the test set, between 0 and 1. Default is 0.2.
    random_seed (Optional[int]): The random seed for reproducibility.

Returns:
    Tuple[pd.DataFrame, pd.DataFrame]: A tuple containing the training and test DataFrames.
        - train_df: Training data with a random subset of interactions.
        - test_df: Test data with the remaining interactions.

functions.name: rtrec.experiments.experiments.run_experiment
documentation:
Run an experiment to evaluate the recommender on the specified dataset.

Args:
    dataset_name (str): Name of the dataset to evaluate the recommender on.
    model_name (str): Name of the model to use for recommendation.
    split_method (str): Method for splitting the data into train and test sets.
    epochs (int): Number of epochs (iterations) over the dataset.
    batch_size (int): Batch size for training the model.

Returns:
    Dict[str, float]: A dictionary of evaluation metrics on the test set.

variables.name: rtrec.utils.collections.T
documentation: A generic type for elements in the SortedSet

methods.name: rtrec.utils.collections.SortedSet.add
documentation:
Adds an item to the sorted set if it's not already present and returns its index.
If the item is already present, returns its existing index.

methods.name: rtrec.utils.collections.SortedSet.index
documentation:
Returns the index of the item in the sorted set.

Returns:
    int: Index of the item in the sorted set. -(insertion point+1) if the item is not found.

functions.name: rtrec.utils.multiprocessing.create_shared_array
documentation:
Create a shared memory segment for a NumPy array and copy the array's data into it.

Args:
    array (np.ndarray): The NumPy array to share.

Returns:
    shared_memory.SharedMemory: The shared memory object containing the array's data.

classes.name: rtrec.utils.identifiers.IdentifierError
documentation: Custom exception for Identifier class key errors.

methods.name: rtrec.utils.identifiers.Identifier.__getitem__
documentation: Allow indexing to access objects by their ID.

modules.name: rtrec.experiments.datasets
documentation: datasets.py

functions.name: rtrec.experiments.datasets.load_movielens
documentation:
Downloads and loads the specified MovieLens dataset version into a DataFrame with columns
user_id, item_id, rating, and tstamp. Optionally loads user and item attributes.

Args:
    dataset_scale (str): The version of the MovieLens dataset to load ('100k', '1m', '10m', '20m', or '25m').
    sort_by_tstamp (bool): Whether to sort the DataFrame by timestamp. Defaults to False.
    load_user_attributes (bool): Whether to load user attributes. Defaults to False.
    load_item_attributes (bool): Whether to load item attributes. Defaults to False.

Returns:
    pd.DataFrame: A DataFrame containing user_id, item_id, rating, and tstamp, with optional attributes.

functions.name: rtrec.experiments.datasets.load_movielens_100k
documentation: Specific functions for loading each dataset version

functions.name: rtrec.experiments.datasets.load_yelp_ratings
documentation:
Loads the Yelp dataset containing user ratings for businesses.

Args:
    sort_by_tstamp (bool): Whether to sort the DataFrame by timestamp. Defaults to False.

Returns:
    pd.DataFrame: A DataFrame containing columns: user, item, rating, and tstamp.

functions.name: rtrec.experiments.datasets.load_amazon_review_v2
documentation:
Downloads and loads the Amazon review dataset for a specified category (or all categories)
and returns it as a pandas DataFrame. Can also load the full dataset or the 5-core subset.

Args:
    category_name (str): Category of the Amazon dataset to load. Defaults to "Music".
                    Use "all" to load all available categories.
    small_subsets (bool): Whether to load the 5-core subset (default is True).
    parse_image_url (bool): Whether to parse image URLs from the dataset (default is False).
    sort_by_tstamp (bool): Whether to sort the DataFrame by timestamp (default is False).

Returns:
    pd.DataFrame: A DataFrame containing user, item, rating, timestamp, image_url, and 
                  (if category is "all") category.

functions.name: rtrec.experiments.datasets.load_lastfm_360k
documentation:
Load the Last.fm 360k dataset containing user interactions with artists.
17,559,530 (18m) interactions from 359,347 (36k) users.

Reference:
- https://zenodo.org/records/6090214
- https://www.upf.edu/web/mtg/lastfm360k

functions.name: rtrec.experiments.datasets.load_dataset
documentation:
Loads a dataset by name.

Args:
    name (str): Name of the dataset to load.

Returns:
    pd.DataFrame: The loaded dataset as a DataFrame.

methods.name: rtrec.utils.features.FeatureStore.put_user_feature
documentation:
Add a list of user features to the user features set.
Replace the existing user features if the user ID already exists.

methods.name: rtrec.utils.features.FeatureStore.put_item_feature
documentation:
Add a list of item features to the item features set.
Replace the existing item features if the item ID already exists.

methods.name: rtrec.utils.features.FeatureStore.get_user_feature_repr
documentation:
Returns:
    csr_matrix: User feature representation of shape (1, n_features)

methods.name: rtrec.utils.features.FeatureStore.get_item_feature_repr
documentation:
Returns:
    csr_matrix: Item feature representation of shape (1, n_features)

methods.name: rtrec.utils.features.FeatureStore.build_user_features_matrix
documentation:
Returns:
    csr_matrix: User features matrix of shape (n_users, n_features)

methods.name: rtrec.utils.features.FeatureStore.build_item_features_matrix
documentation:
Returns:
    csr_matrix: Item features matrix of shape (n_items, n_features)

functions.name: rtrec.utils.math.sigmoid
documentation:
sigmoid function

Parameters
----------
x : array_like, shape=(n_data), dtype=float
    arguments of function

Returns
-------
sig : array, shape=(n_data), dtype=float
    1.0 / (1.0 + exp(- x))

methods.name: rtrec.utils.diskcache.PersistentCache.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the disk-backed storage with an in-memory LRU cache.

```
param filename

Path to the shelve file.

param cache_size

Maximum size of the in-memory LRU cache.

param pickle_protocol

Protocol version for pickling.
```

methods.name: rtrec.utils.diskcache.PersistentCache.get
documentation:
Retrieves a value from the cache or the disk storage.

```
param key

Key to retrieve the value.

return

Value associated with the key, or None if the key is not found.
```

methods.name: rtrec.utils.diskcache.PersistentCache.set
documentation:
Sets a value in the cache. If the cache exceeds its size, the least recently used item is evicted.

```
param key

Key to associate with the value.

param value

Value to store.
```

methods.name: rtrec.utils.diskcache.PersistentCache._evict_if_needed
documentation:
Evicts the least recently used item from the cache if it exceeds the cache size.
The evicted item is saved to disk storage.

methods.name: rtrec.utils.diskcache.PersistentCache.delete
documentation:
Deletes a key-value pair from the cache and the disk storage.

```
param key

Key to delete.
```

methods.name: rtrec.utils.diskcache.PersistentCache.flush
documentation:
Force flush all items in the LRU cache to disk.

```
param clear_lru_cache

If True, clears the LRU cache after flushing.
```

methods.name: rtrec.utils.diskcache.PersistentCache.clear
documentation:
Clears in-memory cache.

```
param memory_only

Whether to clear only the in-memory cache.
```

methods.name: rtrec.utils.diskcache.PersistentCache.close
documentation: Flushes the cache to disk and closes the storage.

methods.name: rtrec.utils.diskcache.PersistentCache.__getitem__
documentation:
Dict-like access interface for getting an item.

```
param key

Key to retrieve the value.
```

methods.name: rtrec.utils.diskcache.PersistentCache.__setitem__
documentation:
Dict-like access interface for setting an item.

```
param key

Key to associate with the value.

param value

Value to store.
```

methods.name: rtrec.utils.diskcache.PersistentCache.__delitem__
documentation:
Dict-like access interface for deleting an item.

```
param key

Key to delete.
```

methods.name: rtrec.utils.diskcache.PersistentCache.__contains__
documentation:
Dict-like access interface for checking if a key exists.

```
param key

Key to check.

return

True if the key exists, False otherwise.
```

methods.name: rtrec.utils.diskcache.PersistentCache.__len__
documentation:
Returns the total size of the cache (LRU cache + disk storage).

```
return

Total number of items in the cache.
```

functions.name: rtrec.utils.metrics.ndcg
documentation:
Computes the normalized Discounted Cumulative Gain (nDCG).

Formula:
    DCG@k = sum(1 / log2(i + 2) * rel(i)) for i in range(k)
    nDCG@k = DCG@k / IDCG@k

where rel(i) = 1 if the item at rank i is relevant, else 0.

Parameters:
    ranked_list: List of recommended items.
    ground_truth: List of relevant items.
    recommend_size: Number of items to recommend.

Returns:
    nDCG score as a float.

functions.name: rtrec.utils.metrics.precision
documentation:
Computes Precision@k.

Formula:
    Precision@k = |relevant ∩ recommended| / k

Parameters:
    ranked_list: List of recommended items.
    ground_truth: List of relevant items.
    recommend_size: Number of items to recommend.

Returns:
    Precision score as a float.

functions.name: rtrec.utils.metrics.recall
documentation:
Computes Recall@k.

Formula:
    Recall@k = |relevant ∩ recommended| / |relevant|

Parameters:
    ranked_list: List of recommended items.
    ground_truth: List of relevant items.
    recommend_size: Number of items to recommend.

Returns:
    Recall score as a float.

functions.name: rtrec.utils.metrics.true_positives
documentation:
Counts the number of true positives.

Formula:
    True Positives@k = |relevant ∩ recommended|

Parameters:
    ranked_list: List of recommended items.
    ground_truth: List of relevant items.
    recommend_size: Number of items to recommend.

Returns:
    Number of true positives as an integer.

functions.name: rtrec.utils.metrics.f1_score
documentation:
Computes F1-score@k, the harmonic mean of precision and recall.

Formula:
    F1@k = 2 * (Precision@k * Recall@k) / (Precision@k + Recall@k)

Parameters:
    ranked_list: List of recommended items.
    ground_truth: List of relevant items.
    recommend_size: Number of items to recommend.

Returns:
    F1-score as a float.

functions.name: rtrec.utils.metrics.hit
documentation:
Computes Hit@k, indicating if at least one relevant item is recommended.

Formula:
    Hit@k = 1 if |relevant ∩ recommended| > 0 else 0

Parameters:
    ranked_list: List of recommended items.
    ground_truth: List of relevant items.
    recommend_size: Number of items to recommend.

Returns:
    Hit score as a float.

functions.name: rtrec.utils.metrics.reciprocal_rank
documentation:
Computes Reciprocal Rank (RR), the reciprocal of the rank of the first relevant item.

Formula:
    RR@k = 1 / rank of first relevant item

Parameters:
    ranked_list: List of recommended items.
    ground_truth: List of relevant items.
    recommend_size: Number of items to recommend.

Returns:
    Reciprocal Rank as a float.

functions.name: rtrec.utils.metrics.mrr
documentation:
Computes Mean Reciprocal Rank (MRR) across multiple queries.

Formula:
    MRR = (1 / |Q|) * sum(RR@k(q) for q in Q)

Parameters:
    ranked_lists: List of recommended lists for each query.
    ground_truths: List of relevant items for each query.
    recommend_size: Number of items to recommend.

Returns:
    MRR score as a float.

functions.name: rtrec.utils.metrics.auc
documentation:
Computes the Area Under the Curve (AUC) for ROC.

Formula:
    AUC@k = #correct positive-negative pairs / #total positive-negative pairs

Parameters:
    ranked_list: List of recommended items.
    ground_truth: List of relevant items.
    recommend_size: Number of items to recommend.

Returns:
    AUC score as a float.

functions.name: rtrec.utils.metrics.average_precision
documentation:
Computes the Average Precision (AP) for a ranked list of items.

Formula:
    AP = (1/R) * Σ (P(k) * rel(k))
where:
    - R is the number of relevant items
    - P(k) is the precision at rank k
    - rel(k) is 1 if the item at rank k is relevant, else 0

Args:
    ranked_list (list): The list of ranked items.
    ground_truth (list): The list of relevant items.
    recommend_size (int): The number of recommendations to consider.

Returns:
    float: The Average Precision score.

functions.name: rtrec.utils.metrics.map_score
documentation:
Computes the Mean Average Precision (MAP) across multiple queries.

Formula:
    MAP = (1 / |Q|) * sum(AP@k(q) for q in Q)

Parameters:
    ranked_lists: List of recommended lists for each query.
    ground_truths: List of relevant items for each query.
    recommend_size: Number of items to recommend.

Returns:
    MAP score as a float.

functions.name: rtrec.utils.metrics.compute_scores
documentation:
Computes batch evaluation metrics across multiple queries.

Parameters:
    evaluation_pairs: Iterable of tuples containing recommended and ground truth items for each query.
    recommend_size: Number of items to recommend.

Returns:
    Dictionary with averaged scores across all queries.

methods.name: rtrec.utils.interactions.UserItemInteractions.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the UserItemInteractions class.

Args:
    min_value (int): Minimum allowable value for interactions.
    max_value (int): Maximum allowable value for interactions.
    decay_rate (Optional[float]): Rate at which interactions decay over time.
                                  If None, no decay is applied.

methods.name: rtrec.utils.interactions.UserItemInteractions.get_decay_rate
documentation:
Retrieves the decay rate for interactions.

Returns:
    Optional[float]: The decay rate for interactions.

methods.name: rtrec.utils.interactions.UserItemInteractions.set_decay_rate
documentation:
Sets the decay rate for interactions.

Args:
    decay_rate (Optional[float]): The decay rate for interactions.

methods.name: rtrec.utils.interactions.UserItemInteractions._apply_decay
documentation:
Applies decay to a given value based on the elapsed time since the last interaction.

Args:
    value (float): The original interaction value.
    last_timestamp (float): The timestamp of the last interaction.

Returns:
    float: The decayed interaction value.

methods.name: rtrec.utils.interactions.UserItemInteractions.add_interaction
documentation:
Adds or updates an interaction count for a user-item pair.

Args:
    user_id (int): ID of the user.
    item_id (int): ID of the item.
    delta (float): Change in interaction count (default is 1.0).
    upsert (bool): Flag to update the interaction count if it already exists (default is False).

methods.name: rtrec.utils.interactions.UserItemInteractions.get_user_item_rating
documentation:
Retrieves the interaction count for a specific user-item pair, applying decay if necessary.

Args:
    user_id (int): ID of the user.
    item_id (int): ID of the item.
    default_rating (float): Default rating to return if no interaction exists (default is 0.0).

Returns:
    float: The decayed interaction value for the specified user-item pair.

methods.name: rtrec.utils.interactions.UserItemInteractions.get_user_items
documentation:
Retrieves the dictionary of item IDs and their interaction counts for a given user,
applying decay to each interaction.

Args:
    user_id (int): ID of the user.
    n_recent (Optional[int]): Number of most recent items to consider (default is None).

Returns:
    List[int]: List of item IDs that the user has interacted with.

methods.name: rtrec.utils.interactions.UserItemInteractions.get_all_item_ids
documentation:
Retrieves a list of all unique item IDs.

Returns:
    List[int]: List of unique item IDs.

methods.name: rtrec.utils.interactions.UserItemInteractions.get_all_users
documentation:
Retrieves a list of all user IDs.

Returns:
    List[int]: List of user IDs.

methods.name: rtrec.utils.interactions.UserItemInteractions.get_all_non_interacted_items
documentation:
Retrieves a list of all items a user has not interacted with.

Args:
    user_id (int): ID of the user.

Returns:
    List[int]: List of item IDs the user has not interacted with.

methods.name: rtrec.utils.interactions.UserItemInteractions.get_all_non_negative_items
documentation:
Retrieves a list of all items with non-negative interaction counts, applying decay to each interaction.

Args:
    user_id (int): ID of the user.

Returns:
    List[int]: List of item IDs with non-negative interaction counts.
